name: 02-mixed-batch
description: /muggle-do invoked with two reviews — one actionable, one ambiguous. Classifies; processes the actionable (push, replies, resolve-reminder); escalates the ambiguous with one terminal message; advances cursor past BOTH; respawns the watcher.

classification.4295962950: ambiguous (only an emoji, no concrete signal)

stages_run: (for actionable) build + unit-tests + ONE E2E + open-prs (push def4560) + per-comment-replies (one on comment 9001) + resolve-reminder
(for ambiguous) emit one terminal escalation message listing review 4295962950 with body '👀 not sure about this'; add 4295962950 to escalated_review_ids

state_writes.last_seen.json: reviewId advanced to 4295962950 (max of input); last_pushed_sha set to def4560; cycles_completed 1→2; pushed_shas appended with def4560; escalated_review_ids now contains 4295962950

side_effects: POST /repos/acme/widget/pulls/142/comments/9001/replies body='Addressed in def4560: extracted into helper.'
gh pr comment 142 body='I addressed these threads in def4560 — mark them resolved when satisfied:\n- #T_1'
expected.notes: The actionable and ambiguous paths run in the SAME /muggle-do invocation. The user sees ONE terminal escalation message (covering all ambiguous reviews in the batch — here just one) AND the reviewer sees the push + replies + resolve-reminder on GitHub. The watcher is respawned regardless — the user clarifies the ambiguous review by submitting a new review on GitHub, which the next watcher tick picks up.
